message.content:
Comme tu peux le voir sur la page workspace, il y a une section de bienvenu qui dit "Bonjour Thomas". Thomas est un prénom par défaut, parce qu'on n'a pas encore initié dans le système l'enregistrement du prénom de l'entité de la personne de l'utilisateur.

Ce qui va se passer, c'est qu'on pourra lors de l'inscription demander le nom de la personne ou de l'entité, si la personne s'inscrit comme une entreprise ou comme une personne physique (un particulier). Admettra le nom qu'elle veut donner à son laboratoire.

Il faudrait également qu'on puisse pour une personne qui a déjà un compte qu'elle puisse changer ce genre d'information dans les paramètres, qu'on peut retrouver en cliquant sur le bouton de l'utilisateur dans le header à droite.

On a le bouton qui permet d'ouvrir un petit menu, notamment pour se rendre dans la bibliothèque et/ou dans les paramètres.

Tu vas changer dans ce menu "Bibliothèque par projet", "Bibliothèque projet" ou "Dossier projet". Celui qui colle le plus avec ce qu'il y a déjà d'implémenté.

Et si on clique sur "Paramètres", effectivement, une page "Paramètres" s'ouvre avec des paramétrages et des configurations de base pour configurer un profil.

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to work on several things related to their application:

1. The welcome section says "Bonjour Thomas" - Thomas is a default name because user name registration hasn't been implemented yet
2. During registration, they want to ask for the person's name or entity name (if registering as a business or individual), and the name they want to give their laboratory
3. Existing users should be able to change this info in settings (accessible via user button in header)
4. Change "Bibliothèque" to something more fitting like "Bibliothèque projet" or "Dossier projet" in the user menu
5. When clicking "Paramètres" (Settings), a settings page should open with basic profile configuration


6. Now I need to explore the codebase to understand the current structure and what's already been implemented.

content.text: Let me start by exploring the codebase to understand the current structure and what's already implemented.

1→import { createServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr'
     2→import { NextResponse, type NextRequest } from 'next/server'
     3→
     4→export async function updateSession(request: NextRequest) {
     5→    let supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({
     6→        request,
     7→    })
     8→
     9→    // Create client with simple cookie handling for middleware
    10→    // Note: Middleware cannot import from src/lib/supabase/server.ts directly due to cookies() usage
    11→    const supabase = createServerClient(
    12→        process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    13→        process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!,
    14→        {
    15→            cookies: {
    16→                getAll() {
    17→                    return request.cookies.getAll()
    18→                },
    19→                setAll(cookiesToSet) {
    20→                    cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
    21→                        request.cookies.set(name, value)
    22→                    )
    23→
    24→                    // Re-create response to update cookies
    25→                    supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({
    26→                        request,
    27→                    })
    28→                    cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
    29→                        supabaseResponse.cookies.set(name, value, options)
    30→                    )
    31→                },
    32→            },
    33→        }
    34→    )
    35→
    36→    // This will refresh session if expired - required for Server Components
    37→    // https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/server-side/nextjs
    38→    await supabase.auth.getUser()
    39→
    40→    return supabaseResponse
    41→}
    42→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1→import { type NextRequest } from 'next/server'
     2→import { updateSession } from '@/lib/supabase/middleware'
     3→
     4→export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
     5→    return await updateSession(request)
     6→}
     7→
     8→export const config = {
     9→    matcher: [
    10→        /*
    11→         * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
    12→         * - _next/static (static files)
    13→         * - _next/image (image optimization files)
    14→         * - favicon.ico (favicon file)
    15→         * Feel free to modify this pattern to include more paths.
    16→         */
    17→        '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)',
    18→    ],
    19→}
    20→

content.content:
This is a [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) project bootstrapped with [`create-next-app`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/cli/create-next-app).

## Getting Started

First, run the development server:

```bash
npm run dev
# or
yarn dev
# or
pnpm dev
# or
bun dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) with your browser to see the result.

You can start editing the page by modifying `app/page.tsx`. The page auto-updates as you edit the file.

This project uses [`next/font`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/optimizing/fonts) to automatically optimize and load [Geist](https://vercel.com/font), a new font family for Vercel.

## Learn More

To learn more about Next.js, take a look at the following resources:

- [Next.js Documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs) - learn about Next.js features and API.
- [Learn Next.js](https://nextjs.org/learn) - an interactive Next.js tutorial.

You can check out [the Next.js GitHub repository](https://github.com/vercel/next.js) - your feedback and contributions are welcome!

## Deploy on Vercel

The easiest way to deploy your Next.js app is to use the [Vercel Platform](https://vercel.com/new?utm_medium=default-template&filter=next.js&utm_source=create-next-app&utm_campaign=create-next-app-readme) from the creators of Next.js.

Check out our [Next.js deployment documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/deploying) for more details.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed report. ## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT ### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW & FRAMEWORK **Technology Stack:** - **Framework**: Next.js 16.1.6 (App Router) - **Language**: TypeScript 5 - **Frontend**: React 19.2.3 with Tailwind CSS 4 - **Backend**: API Routes, Supabase SSR for authentication - **AI Integration**: Anthropic Claude API (claude-opus-4-20250918) - **State Management**: Zustand, XState - **Component Library**: shadcn/ui, Radix UI - **UI Components**: lucide-react (icons), Framer Motion, sonner (toasts) - **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL) **Location**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform` --- ### 2. WORKSPACE PAGE WITH "BONJOUR THOMAS" WELCOME SECTION **File**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/workspace/page.tsx` **Lines 181-182** - Welcome Hero: ```typescript

Bonjour, Thomas.

``` **Key Features** (Lines 37-261): - Client-side component with state management for projects, filters, search, and sorting - Uses localStorage for persistence (`k3rn_projects`, `k3rn_active_project`) - Welcome hero displays statistics: - Line 186: Number of active files (dossiers) - Line 188: Count of validated GO projects - Line 190: Projects needing attention (EXPLORE or KILL) - Grid/List view toggle - Search, filter, and sort functionality - Floating dock for controls (Lines 234-244) - Project detail overlay modal (Lines 247-258) - Archive/unarchive functionality - 5 demo projects included with mock data (Lines 81-87) --- ### 3. HEADER COMPONENT WITH USER BUTTON/MENU **File**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/layout/site-header.tsx` **Header Structure** (Lines 21-104): - Sticky header with backdrop blur - Responsive design with logo and navigation - Two variants: `"public"` and `"app"` **App Navigation** (Lines 42-68): ```typescript const APP_NAV_LINKS = [ { label: "Capture", href: "/capture" }, { label: "Dossiers", href: "/workspace" }, ]; ``` **User Navigation Component**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/layout/user-nav.tsx` **UserNav Features** (Lines 18-73): - Avatar with initials fallback (Line 42) - Dropdown menu with: - **Library link** (Line 58) - navigates to `/workspace` - **Settings link** (Line 62-63) - Currently just a menu item with no navigation - **Logout button** (Line 66) - Calls `signOut()` from auth provider - Language selector available (Line 95) - Sign in button shown when not authenticated (Lines 23-32) **Language Selector**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/ui/language-selector.tsx` (Lines 15-39) - Supports: FR (French), EN (English), ES (Spanish) - Uses Zustand store to persist language preference --- ### 4. REGISTRATION/AUTH FLOW **Auth Provider**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/providers/auth-provider.tsx` **Key Components** (Lines 1-162): - React Context for authentication state - Exports `useAuth()` hook for consuming components - Manages: `user`, `session`, `isLoading`, `showAuthModal` **Methods**: **signIn** (Lines 60-90): - Accepts email and optional password - If password: Uses `signInWithPassword()` from Supabase - Without password: Sends magic link via OTP to `auth/callback` route - Shows success/error toast notifications **signUp** (Lines 92-123): - Similar to signIn - Creates new account via `auth.signUp()` - Requires email confirmation via email redirect **signOut** (Lines 125-137): - Calls `supabase.auth.signOut()` - Redirects to home page `/` **Auth Modal**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/auth/auth-modal.tsx` **Features** (Lines 14-155): - Dialog with tabs for Sign In / Sign Up - Email and password inputs with icons - Form submission handlers (Lines 20-28) - Loading state during auth (Line 15) - Uses Sonner for toast notifications **Middleware**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/middleware.ts` (Lines 1-19) - Manages Supabase session refresh - Runs on all routes except static files and images **Supabase Client Setup**: - **Client-side**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/supabase/client.ts` - **Server-side**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/supabase/server.ts` - **Middleware**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts` **Environment Variables** (`.env.local` Lines 1-6): ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://xdkbgihlubhtwamcukum.supabase.co NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9... OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-proj-... ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-... AI_MODEL=claude-opus-4-20250918 AI_TEMPERATURE=0.2 AI_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 ``` --- ### 5. SETTINGS PAGE **Status**: No dedicated settings page currently exists. The settings menu item in the UserNav (Line 61-63 of user-nav.tsx) is currently non-functional and doesn't navigate anywhere. **Partial Implementation**: - `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/dossier-settings-dialog.tsx` - Only project/dossier settings dialog exists (for editing project title and description) **Project Detail Overlay** (which contains editable parameters): - File: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/project-detail-overlay.tsx` - Lines 211-242: Allows editing of dossier parameters (title, description) - Archive/unarchive/delete functionality available --- ### 6. DATABASE SCHEMA/MODELS **Supabase Setup**: Using PostgreSQL with Supabase SSR adapter **Current Data Model** (Client-side localStorage, not relational DB): **Project/Dossier Interface** (workspace/page.tsx Lines 12-27): ```typescript interface Project { id: string; title?: string; text: string; description?: string; color?: string; globalScore?: number; recommendation?: string; confidenceLevel?: string; viabilityScore?: number; classification?: string; timestamp: string; isDemo?: boolean; isArchived?: boolean; analysis?: any; } ``` **User Model** (from Supabase Auth): ```typescript user: { email: string; user_metadata: { full_name?: string; avatar_url?: string; } } ``` **Analysis Output Schema**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/analysis/types.ts` **AnalysisAxes Interface** (Lines 85-92): ```typescript interface AnalysisAxes { problem: AxisAnalysis; market: AxisAnalysis; differentiation: AxisAnalysis; feasibility: AxisAnalysis; business: AxisAnalysis; founder_fit: AxisAnalysis; } ``` **AnalysisOutput** (Lines 129-139): ```typescript interface AnalysisOutput { meta: AnalysisOutputMeta; idea: AnalysisOutputIdea; analysis: AnalysisAxes; scores: AnalysisScores; decision: AnalysisDecision; risks: AnalysisRisk[]; levers: AnalysisLever[]; enrichment: AnalysisEnrichment; lab_orientation: LabOrientation; } ``` **No actual database tables currently defined** - All project data is stored in browser localStorage at `k3rn_projects`. --- ### 7. I18N & TRANSLATION SETUP **Translation Dictionary**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts` **Supported Locales**: FR (French), EN (English), ES (Spanish) - Type: `Locale = 'FR' | 'EN' | 'ES'` **Language Provider**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/providers/language-provider.tsx` **Features** (Lines 14-35): - React Context with `LanguageProvider` wrapper - `useLanguage()` hook for components - Manages: `currentLang`, `t` (translation object), `setLang()` - Updates `document.documentElement.lang` attribute - Default language: FR (Line 15) **Language Store**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/store/language-store.ts` - Exports `useLanguageStore` as alias for `useLanguage` **Translation Keys Structure** (dictionary.ts): **Common** (Lines 5-12): - appName, ideationMode, analysisPhase, ignite, processing, consulting **Auth** (Lines 13-24): - signIn, signUp, email, password, submit, noAccount, hasAccount, library, settings, logout **Capture** (Lines 25-33): - hero, placeholder, attachFile, addLink, voiceInput, helper, submit **Workspace** (Lines 34-86): - dossiers section: pageTitle, newDossier, search, filter, sort options - dossier details: title, sourceMaterials, research, analysisQuality - canvas and chat sections **Analysis** (Lines 88-191): - Comprehensive analysis terminology - Axes: problem, market, differentiation, feasibility, business, founder_fit - Decisions: GO, EXPLORE, PARK, KILL - Confidence levels: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH - Risk/lever management terms - Demo result structure **Root Layout** (layout.tsx Line 32): ```typescript ``` - Default language set to French --- ### 8. KEY API ROUTES & ENDPOINTS **POST /api/analysis** (Lines 40-217 in route.ts): **Purpose**: Canonical ideation analysis via Claude API **Request Schema** (AnalysisInput): ```json { "meta": { "schema_version": "1.0", "request_id": "uuid", "timestamp": "ISO-8601", "language": "string" }, "idea": { "idea_id": "uuid", "state": "CAPTURED|ANALYZED|ENRICHED" }, "inputs": { "text": { "content": "...", "source": "typed|voice_transcript|imported", "length": number }, "links": [{ "url": "...", "type": "web|social|doc" }], "files": [{ "file_id": "...", "type": "pdf|image|text", "size_kb": number }] }, "context": { "user_id": "...", "experience_level": "BEGINNER|INTERMEDIATE|ADVANCED" } } ``` **Response Schema** (AnalysisOutput): - meta, idea, analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers, enrichment, lab_orientation **Other API Routes**: - POST `/api/enrichment` - Guided enrichment questions - POST `/api/logo-generator/generate` - AI logo generation - POST `/api/logo-generator/refine` - Logo refinement - POST `/api/logo-generator/chat` - Logo discussion --- ### 9. ANALYSIS SCORING SYSTEM **Constants File**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/analysis/constants.ts` **Analysis Axes** (Lines 7-14): 1. problem (20% weight) 2. market (20% weight) 3. differentiation (15% weight) 4. feasibility (15% weight) 5. business (20% weight) 6. founder_fit (10% weight) **Decision Thresholds** (Lines 32-42): - KILL: score < 30 or problem_score < 20 - PARK: score 30-50 or critical axis < 25 - EXPLORE: score 50-70 - GO: score >= 70 (with critical axes >= 50) **Enrichment Triggers** (Lines 45-58): - Axis score < 40 - Global score 50-70 - Max 2 axes, 5 questions per sequence - 90-second timeout --- ### 10. DESIGN SYSTEM & STYLING **Design Tokens**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/globals.css` **Color Palette** (Dark 900 Canvas): - L0 Canvas: #22242B (page background) - L-1 Recessed: #1A1C22 (inputs, insets) - L1 Card: #2C2F38 (elevated cards) - L2 Card Hover: #363940 (interactive) - L3 Surface: #464950 (buttons, controls) - L4 Surface Hi: #62656D (muted interactive) **Accent Color**: #F75105 (K3RN Orange) **Radius Tokens**: - sm: 6px - md: 8px - lg: 12px - xl: 16px - 2xl: 24px (standard card radius) **Typography**: - Font Sans: Inter (from Google Fonts) - Font Mono: JetBrains Mono (from Google Fonts) --- ### 11. COMPONENT TREE STRUCTURE ``` src/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx (Root layout with providers) │ ├── page.tsx (Landing page) │ ├── workspace/ │ │ └── page.tsx (Workspace/Dossiers) │ ├── capture/ │ │ └── page.tsx (Idea capture form) │ ├── canonical-demo/ │ │ └── page.tsx │ └── api/ │ ├── analysis/ (Claude analysis) │ ├── enrichment/ │ └── logo-generator/ ├── components/ │ ├── layout/ │ │ ├── site-header.tsx │ │ └── user-nav.tsx │ ├── auth/ │ │ └── auth-modal.tsx │ ├── workspace/ │ │ ├── pro-card.tsx │ │ ├── project-detail-overlay.tsx │ │ ├── floating-dock.tsx │ │ ├── project-inspector.tsx │ │ ├── canvas-board.tsx │ │ └── expert-chat.tsx │ ├── capture/ │ │ └── analysis-results.tsx │ ├── landing/ │ │ ├── hero.tsx │ │ ├── bento-grid.tsx │ │ └── pricing-membership.tsx │ └── ui/ (shadcn/ui components) ├── providers/ │ ├── auth-provider.tsx │ └── language-provider.tsx ├── store/ │ └── language-store.ts ├── hooks/ │ └── use-speech-recognition.ts └── lib/ ├── supabase/ │ ├── client.ts │ ├── server.ts │ └── middleware.ts ├── analysis/ │ ├── types.ts │ ├── constants.ts │ ├── validators.ts │ ├── scoring.ts │ ├── decision.ts │ ├── enrichment.ts │ └── prompts/ │ └── v1.0/ ├── i18n/ │ └── dictionary.ts └── utils.ts ``` --- ### 12. KEY ENTRY POINTS & FLOW 1. **App Root** → `layout.tsx` wraps with `LanguageProvider` + `AuthProvider` 2. **Home Page** → `/` shows landing page with public header 3. **Workspace** → `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas" + project grid (requires auth state) 4. **Capture** → `/capture` shows idea input form with Claude analysis on submit 5. **Analysis** → POST to `/api/analysis` returns scored decision and enrichment data --- This codebase is a sophisticated Next.js SaaS application for institutional ideation analysis using Claude AI, with strong i18n support, dark mode design system, and modular component architecture.

DATABASE SCHEMA/MODELS **Supabase Setup**: Using PostgreSQL with Supabase SSR adapter **Current Data Model** (Client-side localStorage, not relational DB): **Project/Dossier Interface** (workspace/page.tsx Lines 12-27): ```typescript interface Project { id: string; title?: string; text: string; description?: string; color?: string; globalScore?: number; recommendation?: string; confidenceLevel?: string; viabilityScore?: number; classification?: string; timestamp: string; isDemo?: boolean; isArchived?: boolean; analysis?: any; } ``` **User Model** (from Supabase Auth): ```typescript user: { email: string; user_metadata: { full_name?: string; avatar_url?: string; } } ``` **Analysis Output Schema**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/analysis/types.ts` **AnalysisAxes Interface** (Lines 85-92): ```typescript interface AnalysisAxes { problem: AxisAnalysis; market: AxisAnalysis; differentiation: AxisAnalysis; feasibility: AxisAnalysis; business: AxisAnalysis; founder_fit: AxisAnalysis; } ``` **AnalysisOutput** (Lines 129-139): ```typescript interface AnalysisOutput { meta: AnalysisOutputMeta; idea: AnalysisOutputIdea; analysis: AnalysisAxes; scores: AnalysisScores; decision: AnalysisDecision; risks: AnalysisRisk[]; levers: AnalysisLever[]; enrichment: AnalysisEnrichment; lab_orientation: LabOrientation; } ``` **No actual database tables currently defined** - All project data is stored in browser localStorage at `k3rn_projects`. --- ### 7. I18N & TRANSLATION SETUP **Translation Dictionary**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts` **Supported Locales**: FR (French), EN (English), ES (Spanish) - Type: `Locale = 'FR' | 'EN' | 'ES'` **Language Provider**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/providers/language-provider.tsx` **Features** (Lines 14-35): - React Context with `LanguageProvider` wrapper - `useLanguage()` hook for components - Manages: `currentLang`, `t` (translation object), `setLang()` - Updates `document.documentElement.lang` attribute - Default language: FR (Line 15) **Language Store**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/store/language-store.ts` - Exports `useLanguageStore` as alias for `useLanguage` **Translation Keys Structure** (dictionary.ts): **Common** (Lines 5-12): - appName, ideationMode, analysisPhase, ignite, processing, consulting **Auth** (Lines 13-24): - signIn, signUp, email, password, submit, noAccount, hasAccount, library, settings, logout **Capture** (Lines 25-33): - hero, placeholder, attachFile, addLink, voiceInput, helper, submit **Workspace** (Lines 34-86): - dossiers section: pageTitle, newDossier, search, filter, sort options - dossier details: title, sourceMaterials, research, analysisQuality - canvas and chat sections **Analysis** (Lines 88-191): - Comprehensive analysis terminology - Axes: problem, market, differentiation, feasibility, business, founder_fit - Decisions: GO, EXPLORE, PARK, KILL - Confidence levels: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH - Risk/lever management terms - Demo result structure **Root Layout** (layout.tsx Line 32): ```typescript ``` - Default language set to French --- ### 8. KEY API ROUTES & ENDPOINTS **POST /api/analysis** (Lines 40-217 in route.ts): **Purpose**: Canonical ideation analysis via Claude API **Request Schema** (AnalysisInput): ```json { "meta": { "schema_version": "1.0", "request_id": "uuid", "timestamp": "ISO-8601", "language": "string" }, "idea": { "idea_id": "uuid", "state": "CAPTURED|ANALYZED|ENRICHED" }, "inputs": { "text": { "content": "...", "source": "typed|voice_transcript|imported", "length": number }, "links": [{ "url": "...", "type": "web|social|doc" }], "files": [{ "file_id": "...", "type": "pdf|image|text", "size_kb": number }] }, "context": { "user_id": "...", "experience_level": "BEGINNER|INTERMEDIATE|ADVANCED" } } ``` **Response Schema** (AnalysisOutput): - meta, idea, analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers, enrichment, lab_orientation **Other API Routes**: - POST `/api/enrichment` - Guided enrichment questions - POST `/api/logo-generator/generate` - AI logo generation - POST `/api/logo-generator/refine` - Logo refinement - POST `/api/logo-generator/chat` - Logo discussion --- ### 9. ANALYSIS SCORING SYSTEM **Constants File**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/analysis/constants.ts` **Analysis Axes** (Lines 7-14): 1. problem (20% weight) 2. market (20% weight) 3. differentiation (15% weight) 4. feasibility (15% weight) 5. business (20% weight) 6. founder_fit (10% weight) **Decision Thresholds** (Lines 32-42): - KILL: score < 30 or problem_score < 20 - PARK: score 30-50 or critical axis < 25 - EXPLORE: score 50-70 - GO: score >= 70 (with critical axes >= 50) **Enrichment Triggers** (Lines 45-58): - Axis score < 40 - Global score 50-70 - Max 2 axes, 5 questions per sequence - 90-second timeout --- ### 10. DESIGN SYSTEM & STYLING **Design Tokens**: `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/globals.css` **Color Palette** (Dark 900 Canvas): - L0 Canvas: #22242B (page background) - L-1 Recessed: #1A1C22 (inputs, insets) - L1 Card: #2C2F38 (elevated cards) - L2 Card Hover: #363940 (interactive) - L3 Surface: #464950 (buttons, controls) - L4 Surface Hi: #62656D (muted interactive) **Accent Color**: #F75105 (K3RN Orange) **Radius Tokens**: - sm: 6px - md: 8px - lg: 12px - xl: 16px - 2xl: 24px (standard card radius) **Typography**: - Font Sans: Inter (from Google Fonts) - Font Mono: JetBrains Mono (from Google Fonts) --- ### 11. COMPONENT TREE STRUCTURE ``` src/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx (Root layout with providers) │ ├── page.tsx (Landing page) │ ├── workspace/ │ │ └── page.tsx (Workspace/Dossiers) │ ├── capture/ │ │ └── page.tsx (Idea capture form) │ ├── canonical-demo/ │ │ └── page.tsx │ └── api/ │ ├── analysis/ (Claude analysis) │ ├── enrichment/ │ └── logo-generator/ ├── components/ │ ├── layout/ │ │ ├── site-header.tsx │ │ └── user-nav.tsx │ ├── auth/ │ │ └── auth-modal.tsx │ ├── workspace/ │ │ ├── pro-card.tsx │ │ ├── project-detail-overlay.tsx │ │ ├── floating-dock.tsx │ │ ├── project-inspector.tsx │ │ ├── canvas-board.tsx │ │ └── expert-chat.tsx │ ├── capture/ │ │ └── analysis-results.tsx │ ├── landing/ │ │ ├── hero.tsx │ │ ├── bento-grid.tsx │ │ └── pricing-membership.tsx │ └── ui/ (shadcn/ui components) ├── providers/ │ ├── auth-provider.tsx │ └── language-provider.tsx ├── store/ │ └── language-store.ts ├── hooks/ │ └── use-speech-recognition.ts └── lib/ ├── supabase/ │ ├── client.ts │ ├── server.ts │ └── middleware.ts ├── analysis/ │ ├── types.ts │ ├── constants.ts │ ├── validators.ts │ ├── scoring.ts │ ├── decision.ts │ ├── enrichment.ts │ └── prompts/ │ └── v1.0/ ├── i18n/ │ └── dictionary.ts └── utils.ts ``` --- ### 12. KEY ENTRY POINTS & FLOW 1. **App Root** → `layout.tsx` wraps with `LanguageProvider` + `AuthProvider` 2. **Home Page** → `/` shows landing page with public header 3. **Workspace** → `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas" + project grid (requires auth state) 4. **Capture** → `/capture` shows idea input form with Claude analysis on submit 5. **Analysis** → POST to `/api/analysis` returns scored decision and enrichment data --- This codebase is a sophisticated Next.js SaaS application for institutional ideation analysis using Claude AI, with strong i18n support, dark mode design system, and modular component architecture.

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me clarify a few things with the user before writing the plan. Let me think about what I need to know:

1. The user wants to change "Bibliothèque" in the user menu. Currently `t.auth.library` = "Bibliothèque" and it links to `/workspace`. The user asks me to change it to "Bibliothèque par projet", "Bibliothèque projet" or "Dossier projet" - whichever fits best. Looking at the app, `/workspace` shows "Dossiers" - so "Dossiers" is already the nav label. The library dropdown points to workspace too. 

Since "Dossiers" already appears in the navigation, repeating it in the dropdown would be redundant. "Mes Dossiers" could differentiate it, or "Espace Projet" would provide a distinct label while still being consistent with the project-focused terminology already in the interface. The user specifically gave me three options to choose from, and I notice "Mes Dossiers" is already defined in the i18n translations, which makes it a natural fit that aligns with both the existing nav structure and the personal context the dropdown should convey. "Dossier projet" aligns best with the existing terminology throughout the app. I'm settling on a few key decisions: use that label for consistency, add name and entity fields to registration, and create a dedicated settings page. For storing the profile data, I'll leverage Supabase's user_metadata through the auth.updateUser method since it's the simplest approach without needing a separate database table.

L'alternative serait de créer une table 'profiles' dans Supabase. Quelle approche préfères-tu ?

content.thinking:
The user wants:
1. Table profiles in Supabase - will need migration
2. "Mes Dossiers" for the dropdown label

Now let me think about the full plan. I need to:

1. **Create a `profiles` table in Supabase** - will need SQL migration
2. **Update auth flow** - add profile fields to sign-up (name, entity_type, lab_name)
3. **Create settings page** at `/settings` 
4. **Update workspace welcome** - use profile name instead of "Thomas"
5. **Update user-nav dropdown** - change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers", add navigation to settings


6. **Update i18n dictionary** - add new translation keys

For the profiles table, I'm thinking through the schema with fields like id, display_name, entity_type, lab_name, and timestamps. For the registration flow, I need to decide whether to add all the profile fields directly in the auth modal or create a separate step after sign-up.

I think embedding everything in the sign-up tab keeps things simpler and gives better UX. The settings page should have fields for display name, entity type selector, lab name, and a save button using the app header variant.

For managing profile data, I'm deciding between extending the auth-provider to handle profiles or creating a separate useProfile hook. A dedicated hook feels cleaner for separation of concerns, so I'll create one that fetches and manages profile data from Supabase.

Since they're using Supabase directly without a migrations directory, I'll provide the SQL for table creation and use the Supabase client to interact with it.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for the following features in a Next.js/Supabase app. ## Context - Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Supabase auth - The workspace page at `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas." hardcoded - The user dropdown menu (user-nav.tsx) has "Bibliothèque" → links to /workspace, "Paramètres" → no navigation, "Déconnexion" - Auth uses Supabase with email+password or magic link - i18n dictionary supports FR/EN/ES - No settings page exists yet - User data is only Supabase auth (no profiles table) ## Requirements ### 1. Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

Design a detailed implementation plan for the following features in a Next.js/Supabase app. ## Context - Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Supabase auth - The workspace page at `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas." hardcoded - The user dropdown menu (user-nav.tsx) has "Bibliothèque" → links to /workspace, "Paramètres" → no navigation, "Déconnexion" - Auth uses Supabase with email+password or magic link - i18n dictionary supports FR/EN/ES - No settings page exists yet - User data is only Supabase auth (no profiles table) ## Requirements ### 1. Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for the following features in a Next.js/Supabase app. ## Context - Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Supabase auth - The workspace page at `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas." hardcoded - The user dropdown menu (user-nav.tsx) has "Bibliothèque" → links to /workspace, "Paramètres" → no navigation, "Déconnexion" - Auth uses Supabase with email+password or magic link - i18n dictionary supports FR/EN/ES - No settings page exists yet - User data is only Supabase auth (no profiles table) ## Requirements ### 1. Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for the following features in a Next.js/Supabase app. ## Context - Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Supabase auth - The workspace page at `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas." hardcoded - The user dropdown menu (user-nav.tsx) has "Bibliothèque" → links to /workspace, "Paramètres" → no navigation, "Déconnexion" - Auth uses Supabase with email+password or magic link - i18n dictionary supports FR/EN/ES - No settings page exists yet - User data is only Supabase auth (no profiles table) ## Requirements ### 1. Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for the following features in a Next.js/Supabase app. ## Context - Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Supabase auth - The workspace page at `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas." hardcoded - The user dropdown menu (user-nav.tsx) has "Bibliothèque" → links to /workspace, "Paramètres" → no navigation, "Déconnexion" - Auth uses Supabase with email+password or magic link - i18n dictionary supports FR/EN/ES - No settings page exists yet - User data is only Supabase auth (no profiles table) ## Requirements ### 1. Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

     2→
     3→export function createClient() {
     4→    return createBrowserClient(
     5→        process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
     6→        process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
     7→    )
     8→}
     9→

1→import { createServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr'
     2→import { NextResponse, type NextRequest } from 'next/server'
     3→
     4→export async function updateSession(request: NextRequest) {
     5→    let supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({
     6→        request,
     7→    })
     8→
     9→    // Create client with simple cookie handling for middleware
    10→    // Note: Middleware cannot import from src/lib/supabase/server.ts directly due to cookies() usage
    11→    const supabase = createServerClient(
    12→        process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    13→        process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!,
    14→        {
    15→            cookies: {
    16→                getAll() {
    17→                    return request.cookies.getAll()
    18→                },
    19→                setAll(cookiesToSet) {
    20→                    cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
    21→                        request.cookies.set(name, value)
    22→                    )
    23→
    24→                    // Re-create response to update cookies
    25→                    supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({
    26→                        request,
    27→                    })
    28→                    cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
    29→                        supabaseResponse.cookies.set(name, value, options)
    30→                    )
    31→                },
    32→            },
    33→        }
    34→    )
    35→
    36→    // This will refresh session if expired - required for Server Components
    37→    // https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/server-side/nextjs
    38→    await supabase.auth.getUser()
    39→
    40→    return supabaseResponse
    41→}
    42→

Design a detailed implementation plan for the following features in a Next.js/Supabase app. ## Context - Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Supabase auth - The workspace page at `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas." hardcoded - The user dropdown menu (user-nav.tsx) has "Bibliothèque" → links to /workspace, "Paramètres" → no navigation, "Déconnexion" - Auth uses Supabase with email+password or magic link - i18n dictionary supports FR/EN/ES - No settings page exists yet - User data is only Supabase auth (no profiles table) ## Requirements ### 1. Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

     2→import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"
     3→
     4→export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {

     6→}
     7→

Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Profile hook (`useProfile`) Create a reusable hook that: - Fetches profile data from `profiles` table when user is authenticated - Provides `profile`, `isLoading`, `updateProfile()`, `createProfile()` - Used across workspace, settings, and user-nav ### 3. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update workspace welcome Replace "Bonjour, Thomas." with the user's display_name from profile, or a fallback ### 6. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. Update i18n dictionary Add new keys for all three languages (FR/EN/ES) for: - settings page labels - entity type options - profile-related terms - "Mes Dossiers" replacing "library"/"Bibliothèque" ## Existing Code Context **auth-provider.tsx**: React context with signIn/signUp/signOut, uses Supabase client **auth-modal.tsx**: Dialog with tabs (signin/signup), email+password fields **user-nav.tsx**: Dropdown with avatar, Library link (→/workspace), Settings (no link), Logout **workspace/page.tsx**: Line 181-182: `

Bonjour, Thomas.

` **dictionary.ts**: FR/EN/ES translations, `t.auth.library`, `t.auth.settings` **site-header.tsx**: Nav links: Capture, Dossiers **layout.tsx**: Wraps with LanguageProvider > AuthProvider ## Design Constraints - Keep the existing dark design system (L0 #22242B, L1 #2C2F38, accent #F75105, Inter font) - Follow existing component patterns (shadcn/ui, Radix, lucide-react icons) - Use existing i18n pattern (dictionary + useLanguageStore) - The profile hook should be a Zustand store or a simple React hook - follow existing patterns Please provide a detailed, ordered implementation plan with file paths and key code decisions.

1→import * as React from "react"
     2→
     3→import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
     4→
     5→function Input({ className, type, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"input">) {
     6→  return (
     7→    <input
     8→      type={type}
     9→      data-slot="input"
    10→      className={cn(
    11→        "file:text-foreground placeholder:text-muted-foreground selection:bg-primary selection:text-primary-foreground dark:bg-input/30 border-input h-9 w-full min-w-0 rounded-none border bg-transparent px-3 py-1 text-base transition-[color,box-shadow] outline-none file:inline-flex file:h-7 file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 md:text-sm",
    12→        "focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:ring-[3px]",
    13→        "aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 aria-invalid:border-destructive",
    14→        className
    15→      )}
    16→      {...props}
    17→    />
    18→  )
    19→}
    20→
    21→export { Input }
    22→

1→"use client"
     2→
     3→import * as React from "react"
     4→import { Label as LabelPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
     5→
     6→import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
     7→
     8→function Label({
     9→  className,
    10→  ...props
    11→}: React.ComponentProps<typeof LabelPrimitive.Root>) {
    12→  return (
    13→    <LabelPrimitive.Root
    14→      data-slot="label"
    15→      className={cn(
    16→        "flex items-center gap-2 text-sm leading-none font-medium select-none group-data-[disabled=true]:pointer-events-none group-data-[disabled=true]:opacity-50 peer-disabled:cursor-not-allowed peer-disabled:opacity-50",
    17→        className
    18→      )}
    19→      {...props}
    20→    />
    21→  )
    22→}
    23→
    24→export { Label }
    25→

Design a detailed implementation plan for the following features in a Next.js/Supabase app. ## Context - Next.js 16 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Supabase auth - The workspace page at `/workspace` shows "Bonjour, Thomas." hardcoded - The user dropdown menu (user-nav.tsx) has "Bibliothèque" → links to /workspace, "Paramètres" → no navigation, "Déconnexion" - Auth uses Supabase with email+password or magic link - i18n dictionary supports FR/EN/ES - No settings page exists yet - User data is only Supabase auth (no profiles table) ## Requirements ### 1. Supabase `profiles` table Create a profiles table with: - `id` (UUID, references auth.users.id, PK) - `display_name` (text) - name of person or entity - `entity_type` (text, 'individual' or 'company') - `lab_name` (text) - name they give their laboratory - `created_at` (timestamptz, default now()) - `updated_at` (timestamptz, default now()) Need: SQL migration script, RLS policies (users can only read/update own profile) ### 2. Update registration flow (auth-modal.tsx) In the sign-up tab, add fields: - Display name (text input) - Entity type selector (individual/company toggle or radio) - Lab name (text input) After successful sign-up, create a profile record in the `profiles` table ### 4. Settings page (`/settings`) Create a new page with: - App header (variant="app") - Form to edit: display_name, entity_type, lab_name - Save button that calls updateProfile - Toast on success/error - Consistent dark theme styling (bg-background, cards with #2C2F38, accent #F75105) ### 5. Update user-nav dropdown - Change "Bibliothèque" to "Mes Dossiers" - Add onClick navigation to /settings for the Paramètres menu item ### 7. content.content:
1→import * as React from "react"
     2→import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
     3→import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
     4→
     5→import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
     6→
     7→const buttonVariants = cva(
     8→  "inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 whitespace-nowrap rounded-none text-sm font-medium transition-all disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4 shrink-0 [&_svg]:shrink-0 outline-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:ring-[3px] aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 aria-invalid:border-destructive",
     9→  {
    10→    variants: {
    11→      variant: {
    12→        default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90 hover:shadow-glow hover:-translate-y-[1px] transition-all duration-300",
    13→        destructive:
    14→          "bg-destructive text-white hover:bg-destructive/90 focus-visible:ring-destructive/20 dark:focus-visible:ring-destructive/40 dark:bg-destructive/60",
    15→        outline:
    16→          "border bg-background hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:bg-input/30 dark:border-input dark:hover:bg-input/50",
    17→        secondary:
    18→          "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80",
    19→        ghost:
    20→          "hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground dark:hover:bg-accent/50",
    21→        link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline",
    22→      },
    23→      size: {
    24→        default: "h-9 px-4 py-2 has-[>svg]:px-3",
    25→        xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-none px-2 text-xs has-[>svg]:px-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
    26→        sm: "h-8 rounded-none gap-1.5 px-3 has-[>svg]:px-2.5",
    27→        lg: "h-10 rounded-none px-6 has-[>svg]:px-4",
    28→        icon: "size-9",
    29→        "icon-xs": "size-6 rounded-none [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
    30→        "icon-sm": "size-8",
    31→        "icon-lg": "size-10",
    32→      },
    33→    },
    34→    defaultVariants: {
    35→      variant: "default",
    36→      size: "default",
    37→    },
    38→  }
    39→)
    40→
    41→function Button({
    42→  className,
    43→  variant = "default",
    44→  size = "default",
    45→  asChild = false,
    46→  ...props
    47→}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
    48→  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
    49→    asChild?: boolean
    50→  }) {
    51→  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
    52→
    53→  return (
    54→    <Comp
    55→      data-slot="button"
    56→      data-variant={variant}
    57→      data-size={size}
    58→      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
    59→      {...props}
    60→    />
    61→  )
    62→}
    63→
    64→export { Button, buttonVariants }
    65→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.content:
1→"use client"
     2→
     3→import {
     4→  CircleCheckIcon,
     5→  InfoIcon,
     6→  Loader2Icon,
     7→  OctagonXIcon,
     8→  TriangleAlertIcon,
     9→} from "lucide-react"
    10→import { useTheme } from "next-themes"
    11→import { Toaster as Sonner, type ToasterProps } from "sonner"
    12→
    13→const Toaster = ({ ...props }: ToasterProps) => {
    14→  const { theme = "system" } = useTheme()
    15→
    16→  return (
    17→    <Sonner
    18→      theme={theme as ToasterProps["theme"]}
    19→      className="toaster group"
    20→      icons={{
    21→        success: <CircleCheckIcon className="size-4" />,
    22→        info: <InfoIcon className="size-4" />,
    23→        warning: <TriangleAlertIcon className="size-4" />,
    24→        error: <OctagonXIcon className="size-4" />,
    25→        loading: <Loader2Icon className="size-4 animate-spin" />,
    26→      }}
    27→      style={
    28→        {
    29→          "--normal-bg": "var(--popover)",
    30→          "--normal-text": "var(--popover-foreground)",
    31→          "--normal-border": "var(--border)",
    32→          "--border-radius": "var(--radius)",
    33→        } as React.CSSProperties
    34→      }
    35→      {...props}
    36→    />
    37→  )
    38→}
    39→
    40→export { Toaster }
    41→

     4→export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
     5→    return await updateSession(request)
     6→}
     7→
     8→export const config = {
     9→    matcher: [
    10→        /*
    11→         * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
    12→         * - _next/static (static files)
    13→         * - _next/image (image optimization files)

    15→         * Feel free to modify this pattern to include more paths.
    16→         */
    17→        '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)',
    18→    ],
    19→}

input.content:
# Plan : Profil utilisateur, inscription, paramètres, menu

## Contexte

La page workspace affiche "Bonjour, Thomas." en dur. Il n'y a pas de système de profil utilisateur, pas de page paramètres, et le menu utilisateur a un label "Bibliothèque" à renommer. Ce plan implémente :
- La table `profiles` dans Supabase
- La collecte du nom/type d'entité/nom de labo à l'inscription
- Une page Paramètres pour modifier ces infos
- Le remplacement du message d'accueil par le vrai nom
- Le renommage du menu en "Mes Dossiers" + navigation vers /settings

---

## Fichiers à créer

### 1. `supabase/migrations/001_create_profiles.sql`
Migration SQL pour créer la table `profiles` avec :
- `id` UUID (PK, FK → auth.users, CASCADE)
- `display_name` TEXT
- `entity_type` TEXT (CHECK: 'individual' | 'company')
- `lab_name` TEXT
- `created_at`, `updated_at` TIMESTAMPTZ
- RLS : SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE pour `auth.uid() = id`
- Trigger `handle_updated_at()` pour auto-maj du `updated_at`

### 2. `src/lib/types/profile.ts`
Types TypeScript : `Profile` et `ProfileUpdate`

### 3. `src/hooks/use-profile.ts`
Hook React `useProfile()` :
- Fetch le profil depuis Supabase quand `user` change
- Expose `profile`, `isLoading`, `createProfile()`, `updateProfile()`
- Gère le cas "pas encore de profil" (PGRST116)

### 4. `src/app/settings/page.tsx`
Page Paramètres avec :
- Header app (`SiteHeader variant="app"`)
- Formulaire : display_name, entity_type (toggle), lab_name
- Bouton sauvegarder, toasts succès/erreur
- Style cohérent (card L1 #2C2F38, inputs existants, accent #F75105)

---

## Fichiers à modifier

### 5. `src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts`
Ajouts dans les 3 locales (FR/EN/ES) :
- `auth.myDossiers` : "Mes Dossiers" / "My Dossiers" / "Mis Expedientes"
- `auth.displayName`, `auth.entityType`, `auth.entityIndividual`, `auth.entityCompany`, `auth.labName`
- Nouvelle section `settings` : title, subtitle, profileSection, save, saving, saved, error, placeholders
- `workspace.welcome` : "Bonjour" / "Hello" / "Hola"
- `workspace.defaultUser` : "Utilisateur" / "User" / "Usuario"

### 6. `src/providers/auth-provider.tsx`
- Étendre `signUp()` pour accepter `profileData` (display_name, entity_type, lab_name)
- Passer les données profil dans `options.data` de `supabase.auth.signUp()` (stockées dans user_metadata)
- Dans `onAuthStateChange`, quand `SIGNED_IN` : auto-créer le profil si absent (depuis user_metadata)

### 7. `src/components/auth/auth-modal.tsx`
Ajouter dans l'onglet inscription :
- Champ "Nom d'affichage" (input texte avec icône User)
- Sélecteur "Type d'entité" (toggle boutons Individuel/Entreprise)
- Champ "Nom du laboratoire" (input texte avec icône Flask/Beaker)
- Appel à `signUp(email, password, { display_name, entity_type, lab_name })`

### 8. `src/app/workspace/page.tsx`
- Importer `useProfile` et `useLanguageStore`
- Remplacer `Bonjour, Thomas.` par `{t.workspace.welcome}, {profile?.display_name || t.workspace.defaultUser}.`

### 9. `src/components/layout/user-nav.tsx`
- Importer `useProfile`, changer icône Library → FolderOpen
- Afficher `profile?.display_name` dans le label du dropdown
- Changer `t.auth.library` → `t.auth.myDossiers`
- Ajouter `onClick={() => router.push("/settings")}` sur le menu item Paramètres

---

## Ordre d'implémentation

1. SQL migration + type Profile (pas de dépendances)
2. Hook useProfile (dépend du type)
3. Dictionnaire i18n (pas de dépendances)
4. auth-provider.tsx (dépend de la table profiles)
5. auth-modal.tsx (dépend du auth-provider modifié + i18n)
6. settings page (dépend du hook + i18n)
7. workspace page (dépend du hook + i18n)
8. user-nav (dépend du hook + i18n)

---

## Vérification

1. Exécuter la migration SQL dans Supabase (Dashboard ou CLI)
2. `npm run dev` - vérifier que l'app compile sans erreur TypeScript
3. Tester l'inscription : les 3 champs apparaissent, le profil est créé en BDD
4. Tester la page /settings : le formulaire charge les données, sauvegarde fonctionne
5. Vérifier le workspace : "Bonjour, [nom]." s'affiche correctement
6. Vérifier le menu utilisateur : "Mes Dossiers" et navigation vers /settings

input.content:
-- Create profiles table
CREATE TABLE public.profiles (
  id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE PRIMARY KEY,
  display_name TEXT,
  entity_type TEXT CHECK (entity_type IN ('individual', 'company')) DEFAULT 'individual',
  lab_name TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now() NOT NULL
);

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE public.profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Policy: users can read only their own profile
CREATE POLICY "Users can read own profile"
  ON public.profiles
  FOR SELECT
  USING (auth.uid() = id);

-- Policy: users can insert their own profile
CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own profile"
  ON public.profiles
  FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = id);

-- Policy: users can update only their own profile
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own profile"
  ON public.profiles
  FOR UPDATE
  USING (auth.uid() = id)
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = id);

-- Auto-update updated_at on row change
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER on_profiles_updated
  BEFORE UPDATE ON public.profiles
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION public.handle_updated_at();

content.text:
Le build compile sans erreur. Voici un récapitulatif de tout ce qui a été fait :

## Fichiers créés (4)

- **`supabase/migrations/001_create_profiles.sql`** -- Migration SQL pour la table `profiles` avec RLS et trigger `updated_at`
- **`src/lib/types/profile.ts`** -- Types `Profile` et `ProfileUpdate`
- **`src/hooks/use-profile.ts`** -- Hook `useProfile()` pour fetch/create/update le profil depuis Supabase
- **`src/app/settings/page.tsx`** -- Page Parametres avec formulaire (nom, type d'entite, nom du labo)

## Fichiers modifies (4)

- **`src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts`** -- Ajout de `myDossiers`, `displayName`, `entityType`, `entityIndividual`, `entityCompany`, `labName` dans `auth`, nouvelle section `settings`, et `welcome`/`defaultUser` dans `workspace` pour les 3 locales (FR/EN/ES)
- **`src/providers/auth-provider.tsx`** -- `signUp()` accepte maintenant `profileData` et le stocke dans `user_metadata`. `onAuthStateChange` auto-cree le profil dans la table `profiles` au premier login.
- **`src/components/auth/auth-modal.tsx`** -- L'onglet inscription a 3 nouveaux champs : nom d'affichage, type de compte (toggle Particulier/Entreprise), nom du laboratoire
- **`src/components/layout/user-nav.tsx`** -- "Bibliotheque" renomme en "Mes Dossiers" (icone FolderOpen), affichage du `display_name` du profil, navigation vers `/settings` sur le clic Parametres
- **`src/app/workspace/page.tsx`** -- "Bonjour, Thomas." remplace par `{t.workspace.welcome}, {profile?.display_name || t.workspace.defaultUser}.`

## Prochaine etape requise

Tu dois executer la migration SQL (`supabase/migrations/001_create_profiles.sql`) sur ta base Supabase, soit via le Dashboard SQL Editor, soit via `supabase db push` si tu utilises le CLI.
